intro: Eugenioides juergensenii, also known as Eugenia juergensenii, is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the states of Oaxaca and Chiapas in Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its potential health benefits in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eugenia juergensenii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
